assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been recognised as a foreign-currency receivable/payable between group entities with different functional currencies.
For this hedge analysis, that recognised dividend balance is treated as an intragroup monetary item whose foreign exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup foreign-currency receivable/payable can be considered under the consolidated-accounts exception for intragroup monetary items and therefore is relevant to assess as a fair value hedge.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also defines cash flow hedges for variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability, so this is a distinct hedge accounting model to consider for the foreign-currency receivable.
